course_title: Tools and Techniques for Business Analysis

learning_outcomes.description: Analyse real-world business problems using relevant analytical tools.
Collaboratively evaluate business decisions from different perspectives.
Recommend strategies to balance the needs of shareholders and other stakeholders.
Justify positions supported by evidence and respect for the audience.
Reflect on personal assumptions and values to question business norms and challenge the status quo.

assessments.task_description: This assessment will be an invigilated, in-person exam. Part A is multichoice (20 marks) and Part B is a written response to a short business scenario(10 marks).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: Students will be formed into teams in their Week 4 tutorial. In Week 6 the team will need to independently organise a visit to one of the three branches of the case study organisation- Bunnings. As part of this visit, teams are expected to complete the 'site visit template' in order to familiarise themselves with the case study. This submission is due at the beginning of Week 7. Students will be graded on the completeness of the template.
assessments.submission_guidelines: One team member should submit the completed template via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Boardroom Roleplay 1

Boardroom Roleplay 2

Boardroom Roleplay 3

In-person during allocated workshops.
assessments.task_description:
Students will have three opportunities to engage in a boardroom roleplay in their allocated tutorial with their team. These assessed tutorials will be held in Weeks 7, 8, and 9 with the best two (2) marks of the three (3) submissions contributing to their grade. The boardroom roleplay is worth 15 marks, totalling 30 marks (2 x 15 marks).

This is a secure assessment and no devices are allowed. Students must bring a pen and may bring one page of notes, a printed copy of the case study and a printed copy of the lecture slides to the tutorial.

Teams are expected to provide peer evaluation data at the end of each of these three tutorials. While this is a team-based task, adjustments may be made to individual student marks based on the quality of their individual section and/or their performance within the boardroom roleplay. These adjustments may be made on the basis of peer evaluation data and/or teaching team observations.
assessments.submission_guidelines: At the end of the workshop, one team member should take a photo of the team worksheet and submit this via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students will be provided three in-class reflective tasks that will inform the development of a 5-minute video submission. Each reflective task will be discussed and developed in the final workshops of the course and will draw upon course concepts and themes and student's own personal experience. These reflections should be synthesised and distilled into a 5-minute business leadership manifesto that should be recorded as a video and demonstrate professional communication. This video should be addressed to the student's future self and outline what kind of business leader the student would like to be.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
